Text: While you scroll, let's revise this for your next interview.
Day 3 of turning you into a logic machine.
Today's weapon, fast and slow pointers.
This pattern looks simple, and that's why people miss it.
You're given a linked list or a sequence,
If there's a cycle, fast will eventually catch slow.
Because fast is literally chasing slow.
If there's no cycle, fast hits the end.
Want the middle of a list?
By the time fast reaches the end, slow is already at the middle.
This drops complexity to O of end time and O of one space.
Once this clicks, linked list questions stop being scary.
